methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Carbyon is a Series A climate and energy technology company founded by Hans De Neve, having raised €22.3M to date. The valuation universe comprises nine peers in the Climate & Energy sector: one public company (Linzia, ticker LNZA) and eight private firms including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

Peer-median valuation methodology proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables. The filtered peer set—restricted to same-stage Series A companies, adjacent-stage rounds, or similar valuation tiers within Climate & Energy—yielded fewer than three datapoints across all three filter criteria. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias given the heterogeneity of subsectors and technology maturities within Climate & Energy. Consequently, no triangulated valuation range can be defensibly derived from multiples-based approaches alone.

The absence of reliable public market anchors (one public comp with limited comparability) and the fragmentation of private funding rounds across dissimilar subsectors and capital structures limits precision in this analysis. A credible valuation would require either: additional Series A Climate & Energy comparables with disclosed post-money valuations; detailed financial metrics (revenue, burn, unit economics) for peer benchmarking; or primary information on Carbyon's specific subsector positioning, technology stage, and capital efficiency relative to the identified peer set. Further data collection is necessary before a supported valuation range can be provided.
